source: Learning to Simulate Complex Physics with Graph Networks.pdf

concepts.finding: Graph networks that model pairwise interactions between particles can learn to simulate complex physical systems, generalizing to unseen initial conditions and system sizes with 95%+ accuracy.
concepts.implication: Graph-based relational reasoning (modeling interactions between entities) can transfer to search: modeling query-document, query-query, and document-document relationships as a graph.
concepts.open_question: Can graph network simulations of user-behavior dynamics predict long-term search session outcomes?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A graph network modeling query-document interactions in a session will predict next-click behavior more accurately than sequential models by 10%+ AUC.
